model.smoothcomp_warehouse.absolute_even.name: absolute_event_stats
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.absolute_even.description: Performance metrics grouped by an athlete's absolute experience cohort, measuring how results change as athletes progress in their careers.

absolute_cohort.name: absolute_cohort
absolute_cohort.description: Career progression cohort for the athlete.

total_matches.description: Total matches fought by athletes in the cohort.

total_athletes.name: total_athletes
total_athletes.description: Number of unique athletes in the cohort.

win_rate.name: win_rate
win_rate.description: Share of matches won by athletes in the cohort.

submission_win_rate.name: submission_win_rate
submission_win_rate.description: Share of matches won via submission.

submission_loss_rate.description: Share of losses occurring via submission.

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.absolute_even.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized='table',
        format='parquet'
    )
}}

WITH aggregate AS (
    SELECT
        absolute_cohort,
        /* divide by 2 for two competitors per match */
        SUM(total_matches) / 2 AS total_matches,
        SUM(wins) / 2 AS wins,
        SUM(submission_wins) / 2 AS submission_wins,
        SUM(losses) / 2 AS losses,
        SUM(submission_losses) / 2 AS submission_losses
    FROM {{ ref('athlete_event_index') }} e
    JOIN {{ ref('athlete_event_absolute_cohort') }} c
        ON e.athlete_id = c.athlete_id
    GROUP BY
        absolute_cohort
)
SELECT
    absolute_cohort,
    {{ round_val('total_matches', 0) }} AS total_matches,
    {{ round_val('CAST(wins AS DOUBLE) / total_matches', 3) }} AS win_rate,
    {{ round_val('CAST(submission_wins AS DOUBLE) / total_matches', 3) }} AS submission_win_rate,
    {{ round_val('CAST(submission_losses AS DOUBLE) / total_matches', 3) }} AS submission_loss_rate
FROM aggregate

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.club_event_st.name: club_event_stats
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.club_event_st.description: Performance statistics segmented by the number of teammates present at the same event, used to evaluate the impact of team size on outcomes.

teammates_cohort.name: teammates_cohort
teammates_cohort.description: Bucket representing number of teammates attending the event.

total_matches.name: total_matches
total_matches.description: Total matches fought within the cohort.

submission_win_rate.name: submission_win_rate
submission_win_rate.description: Share of matches won via submission.

submission_share_of_wins.name: submission_share_of_wins
submission_share_of_wins.description: Percentage of wins that occurred via submission.

submission_share_of_losses.name: submission_share_of_losses
submission_share_of_losses.description: Percentage of losses that occurred via submission.

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.club_event_st.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized = 'table',
        format = 'parquet'
    )
}}
SELECT
    teammates_cohort,

    COUNT(*) / 2 AS total_matches,
    SUM(wins) / 2 AS wins,
    SUM(losses) / 2 AS losses,
    SUM(submission_wins) / 2 AS submission_wins,
    SUM(submission_losses) / 2 AS submission_losses,

    CAST(SUM(wins) AS DOUBLE) / COUNT(*) AS win_rate,
    CAST(SUM(submission_wins) AS DOUBLE) / COUNT(*) AS submission_win_rate,
    CAST(SUM(submission_losses) AS DOUBLE) / COUNT(*) AS submission_loss_rate,

    CAST(SUM(submission_wins) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(SUM(wins),0) 
        AS submission_share_of_wins,

    CAST(SUM(submission_losses) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(SUM(losses),0) 
        AS submission_share_of_losses
FROM (
    SELECT
        wins,
        losses,
        submission_wins,
        submission_losses,
        CASE
            WHEN teammates_at_event = 0 THEN '0'
            WHEN teammates_at_event = 1 THEN '1'
            WHEN teammates_at_event <= 3 THEN '2-3'
            WHEN teammates_at_event <= 7 THEN '4-7'
            WHEN teammates_at_event <= 15 THEN '8-15'
            WHEN teammates_at_event <= 31 THEN '16-31'
            WHEN teammates_at_event <= 63 THEN '32-63'
            ELSE '64+'
        END AS teammates_cohort

    FROM {{ ref('athlete_event_club_index') }}

) t

GROUP BY teammates_cohort

ORDER BY
    CASE teammates_cohort
        WHEN '0' THEN 1
        WHEN '1' THEN 2
        WHEN '2-3' THEN 3
        WHEN '4-7' THEN 4
        WHEN '8-15' THEN 5
        WHEN '16-31' THEN 6
        ELSE 7
    END

prior_matches_cohort.description: Bucket of matches fought in the previous 180 days.

submission_win_rate.description: Share of matches won via submission.

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.matches_perio.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized='table',
        format='parquet'
    )
}}
SELECT
    prior_matches_cohort,

    COUNT(*) / 2 AS total_matches,                  -- divide by 2 to account for double rows
    SUM(win) / 2 AS wins,
    SUM(submission_win) / 2 AS submission_wins,
    SUM(loss) / 2 AS losses,
    SUM(submission_loss) / 2 AS submission_losses,

    CAST(SUM(win) / 2 AS DOUBLE) / (COUNT(*) / 2) AS win_rate,
    CAST(SUM(submission_win) / 2 AS DOUBLE) / (COUNT(*) / 2) AS submission_win_rate,
    CAST(SUM(submission_loss) / 2 AS DOUBLE) / (COUNT(*) / 2) AS submission_loss_rate

FROM (
    SELECT
        athlete_id,
        match_index,
        win,
        submission_win,
        loss,
        submission_loss,

        CASE
            WHEN prior_matches_180 < 1 THEN '0'
            WHEN prior_matches_180 = 1 THEN '1'
            WHEN prior_matches_180 <= 3 THEN '2-3'
            WHEN prior_matches_180 <= 7 THEN '4-7'
            WHEN prior_matches_180 <= 15 THEN '8-15'
            WHEN prior_matches_180 <= 31 THEN '16-31'
            WHEN prior_matches_180 <= 63 THEN '32-63'
            ELSE '64+'
        END AS prior_matches_cohort

    FROM {{ ref('matches_by_athlete_period') }}
) t

GROUP BY prior_matches_cohort

ORDER BY
    CASE prior_matches_cohort
        WHEN '0' THEN 1
        WHEN '1' THEN 2
        WHEN '2-3' THEN 3
        WHEN '4-7' THEN 4
        WHEN '8-15' THEN 5
        WHEN '16-31' THEN 6
        WHEN '32-63' THEN 7
        WHEN '64-127' THEN 8
        ELSE 9
    END

}}

        /* divide by 2 for two competitors per match */
        sum(total_matches) / 2 as total_matches,
        sum(wins) / 2 as wins,
        sum(submission_wins) / 2 as submission_wins,

        sum(submission_losses) / 2 as submission_losses

    WHERE

    GROUP BY
        event_index_grouping
)
SELECT
    *,

    {{ round_val('CAST(submission_losses AS DOUBLE) / total_matches', 3) }} AS submission_loss_rate
FROM
    BASE
WHERE
    event_index_grouping is not null
ORDER BY
    event_index_grouping asc

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.events_matche.name: events_matches_by_month
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.events_matche.description: Monthly counts of events and matches on the platform with 12-month rolling averages used to analyze growth trends over time.

event_count.name: event_count
event_count.description: Number of unique events held during the month.

match_count.name: match_count
match_count.description: Total matches recorded across events in the month.

event_rolling_avg_12mo.name: event_rolling_avg_12mo
event_rolling_avg_12mo.description: 12-month rolling average of monthly event counts.

match_rolling_avg_12mo.name: match_rolling_avg_12mo
match_rolling_avg_12mo.description: 12-month rolling average of monthly match counts.

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.events_matche.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized = 'table',
        format = 'parquet'
    )
}}
WITH monthly_counts AS (
    SELECT
        event_month,
        COUNT(DISTINCT id) AS event_count,
        sum(match_count)   AS match_count
    FROM {{ ref('events') }}
    GROUP BY 1
),

monthly_rolling AS (
    SELECT
        event_month,
        event_count,
        match_count,
        AVG(event_count) OVER (
            ORDER BY event_month
            ROWS BETWEEN 11 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
        ) AS event_rolling_avg_12mo,
        AVG(match_count) OVER (
            ORDER BY event_month
            ROWS BETWEEN 11 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
        ) AS match_rolling_avg_12mo
    FROM monthly_counts
)

SELECT *
FROM monthly_rolling
ORDER BY event_month

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.matches_by_ag.name: matches_by_age_dist
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.matches_by_ag.description: Distribution of matches across age divisions and styles, including the percentage share of matches within each age group.

match_count.name: match_count
match_count.description: Number of matches recorded for the age and style grouping.

pct_within_age.name: pct_within_age
pct_within_age.description: Percentage of matches within the age division represented by this style.

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.matches_by_ag.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized='table',
        format='parquet'
    )
}}
WITH match_counts as (
    SELECT
        age,
        style,
        count(distinct match_id) as match_count
    FROM {{ ref('matches') }} m
    WHERE
        age   <> 'NA' and 
        style <> 'NA'
    GROUP BY
        age,
        style
    ORDER BY
        age,
        style
),
totals AS (
    SELECT
        age,
        style,
        match_count,
        SUM(match_count) OVER (PARTITION BY age) AS age_total
    FROM match_counts
)
SELECT
    age,
    style,
    match_count,
    match_count * 1.0 / age_total AS pct_within_age
FROM totals
ORDER BY
    CASE age
        WHEN 'Youth' THEN 1
        WHEN 'Adult' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Master' THEN 3
    END,
    style

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.name: athlete_event_absolute_cohort
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.description:
Cohort classification based on how frequently an athlete competes
in Absolute divisions.

Athletes are grouped into frequency cohorts based on the number
of events where they entered Absolute brackets.

**Grain:** One row per athlete.

**Upstream models:**
- matches
- athlete_event_index
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized = 'table',
        format = 'parquet'
    )
}}
WITH abs_counts AS (

    SELECT
        athlete_id,
        COUNT(distinct event_id) AS absolute_events
    FROM {{ ref('matches') }}
    WHERE skill = 'Absolute'
    GROUP BY athlete_id
)

SELECT
    a.athlete_id,
    CASE
        WHEN absolute_events IS NULL THEN 'Never'
        WHEN absolute_events = 1     THEN 'Once'
        WHEN absolute_events <= 3    THEN '2-3'
        ELSE 'More than 3'
    END AS absolute_cohort
FROM {{ ref('athlete_event_index') }} a
LEFT JOIN abs_counts b
    ON a.athlete_id = b.athlete_id
GROUP BY
    a.athlete_id,
    absolute_events

)

        ELSE 'More than 3'
    END AS absolute_cohort
FROM "dev"."main_silver"."athlete_event_index" a
LEFT JOIN abs_counts b
    ON a.athlete_id = b.athlete_id
GROUP BY

    absolute_events

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.name: athlete_event_style_cohort
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.description:
Cohort classification describing an athlete's competition style
preference between Gi and No-Gi events.

The cohort is determined by the ratio of Gi events to total
events participated in.

**Grain:** One row per athlete.

**Upstream models:**
- matches
- athlete_event_index
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized = 'table',
        format = 'parquet'
    )
}}
WITH style_counts AS (

    SELECT
        athlete_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN style = 'Gi' THEN event_id END)    AS gi_events,
        COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN style = 'No Gi' THEN event_id END) AS nogi_events
    FROM {{ ref('matches') }}
    GROUP BY athlete_id

),

ratios AS (

    SELECT
        athlete_id,
        gi_events,
        nogi_events,
        gi_events + nogi_events AS total_events,
        CAST(gi_events AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(gi_events + nogi_events, 0) AS gi_ratio
    FROM style_counts

)

SELECT
    a.athlete_id,

    CASE
        WHEN gi_ratio IS NULL
            THEN NULL

        WHEN gi_ratio = 1
            THEN 'Gi Specialist'

        WHEN gi_ratio >= 0.6
            THEN 'Mostly Gi'

        WHEN gi_ratio >= 0.4
            THEN 'Balanced'

        WHEN gi_ratio > 0
            THEN 'Mostly No Gi'

        WHEN gi_ratio = 0
            THEN 'No Gi Specialist'

    END AS style_cohort

FROM {{ ref('athlete_event_index') }} a
LEFT JOIN ratios r
    ON a.athlete_id = r.athlete_id

GROUP BY
    a.athlete_id,
    gi_ratio

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.name: athlete_event_career_length
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.description:
Calculates the career duration of athletes at each event relative
to their first recorded competition.

**Grain:** One row per athlete per event.

**Metric produced:**
- career length in days since first competition.

**Upstream model:**
- matches
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized = 'table',
        format = 'parquet'
    )
}}
WITH career_calc AS (
    SELECT
        athlete_id,
        event_id,
        date,

        MIN(date) OVER (
            PARTITION BY athlete_id
        ) AS first_comp_date,

        date_diff(
            'day',
            MIN(date) OVER (PARTITION BY athlete_id),
            date
        ) AS career_length_days
    FROM {{ ref('matches') }}
)

SELECT DISTINCT
    athlete_id,
    event_id,
    date,
    first_comp_date,
    career_length_days
FROM career_calc
ORDER BY
    athlete_id,
    career_length_days ASC

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.name: athlete_event_club_index
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.description:
Adds club context to athlete event records by calculating the
number of teammates from the same club competing in the same event.

This can be used to analyze the effect of team presence on
athlete performance.

**Grain:** One row per athlete per event.

**Upstream models:**
- athlete_match_index
- matches
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized = 'table',
        format = 'parquet'
    )
}}
WITH athlete_event_club AS (

    -- Get one club per athlete per event
    SELECT
        athlete_id,
        event_id,
        ANY_VALUE(club) AS club
    FROM {{ ref('matches') }}
    WHERE club IS NOT NULL 
    GROUP BY
        athlete_id,
        event_id

),

club_event_counts AS (

    SELECT
        event_id,
        club,
        COUNT(*) AS club_athletes_at_event
    FROM athlete_event_club
    GROUP BY
        event_id,
        club

),

club_size AS (
    SELECT
        a.athlete_id,
        a.event_id,
        a.club,

        COALESCE(c.club_athletes_at_event - 1, 0) AS teammates_at_event

    FROM athlete_event_club a

    LEFT JOIN club_event_counts c
        ON a.event_id = c.event_id
        AND a.club = c.club
)

SELECT
    m.*,
    c.teammates_at_event

FROM {{ ref('athlete_match_index') }} m

LEFT JOIN club_size c
    ON m.athlete_id = c.athlete_id
    AND m.event_id = c.event_id

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.matches_by_at.name: matches_by_athlete_period
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.matches_by_at.description:
Rolling performance metrics for athletes based on recent match history.

The model calculates match outcomes in prior time windows
relative to each match.

Windows included:
- Previous 180 days
- Previous 360 days

These features are useful for:
- performance trend analysis
- predictive modeling
- athlete momentum analysis

**Grain:** One row per athlete per match.

**Upstream models:**
- matches
- athlete_match_index
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.matches_by_at.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized='table',
        format='parquet'
    )
}}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        m.athlete_id,
        m.match_id,
        mi.match_index,
        m.date,
        m.win,
        m.submission_win,
        m.loss,
        m.submission_loss
    FROM {{ ref('matches') }} m
    LEFT JOIN {{ ref('athlete_match_index') }} mi
        ON mi.athlete_id = m.athlete_id
       AND mi.match_id = m.match_id
)

SELECT
    b1.athlete_id,
    b1.match_index,
    b1.date,

    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN b2.date > b1.date - INTERVAL '180' DAY THEN b2.win + b2.loss END), 0) AS prior_matches_180,
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN b2.date > b1.date - INTERVAL '180' DAY THEN b2.win END), 0) AS prior_win_180,
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN b2.date > b1.date - INTERVAL '180' DAY THEN b2.submission_win END), 0) AS prior_submission_win_180,
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN b2.date > b1.date - INTERVAL '180' DAY THEN b2.loss END), 0) AS loss_180,
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN b2.date > b1.date - INTERVAL '180' DAY THEN b2.submission_loss END), 0) AS prior_submission_loss_180,

    COALESCE(SUM(b2.win + b2.loss), 0) AS prior_matches_360,
    COALESCE(SUM(b2.win), 0) AS prior_win_360,
    COALESCE(SUM(b2.submission_win), 0) AS prior_submission_win_360,
    COALESCE(SUM(b2.loss), 0) AS loss_360,
    COALESCE(SUM(b2.submission_loss), 0) AS prior_submission_loss_360,

    b1.win,
    b1.submission_win,
    b1.loss,
    b1.submission_loss

FROM base b1

LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT *
    FROM base b2
    WHERE b2.athlete_id = b1.athlete_id
      AND (
           b2.date > b1.date - INTERVAL '360' DAY  -- in the last 360 days
           AND (b2.date < b1.date 
                OR (b2.date = b1.date AND b2.match_index < b1.match_index))
          )
) b2 ON TRUE

--WHERE b1.athlete_id = 3613

GROUP BY
    b1.athlete_id,
    b1.match_index,
    b1.date,
    b1.win,
    b1.submission_win,
    b1.loss,
    b1.submission_loss

ORDER BY
    b1.athlete_id,
    b1.match_index

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.name: athlete_event_index
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.description:
Core athlete event progression table that indexes each event in an
athlete's competition history.

This model calculates the order of events for each athlete and
aggregates match outcomes within each event.

**Grain:** One row per athlete per event.

**Purpose:**
- Establishes a chronological event index for athlete careers.
- Provides event-level performance metrics.
- Serves as a base model for multiple progression analyses.

**Upstream model:**
- matches

event_index.name: event_index
event_index.description: Sequential number of the event in the athlete's career.

event_index_grouping.name: event_index_grouping
event_index_grouping.description: Bucketed version of event index for cohort analysis.

total_matches.name: total_matches
total_matches.description: Total matches for the athlete in the event.

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.athlete_event.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized = 'table',
        format = 'parquet'
    )
}}
With event_indexed as (
    SELECT
        event_id,
        athlete_id,
        date,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER ( 
            PARTITION BY athlete_id
            ORDER BY date
        ) as event_index,
        sum(win)+sum(loss) as total_matches,
        sum(win) as wins,
        sum(submission_win) as submission_wins,
        sum(loss) as losses,
        sum(submission_loss) as submission_losses
    FROM 
        {{ ref('matches')}}        
    GROUP BY 
        event_id,
        athlete_id,
        date
)
SELECT
    athlete_id,
    event_id,
    date,
    event_index,
    CASE
        WHEN event_index <= 1   then '1st'
        when event_index <= 2   then '2nd'
        WHEN event_index <= 4   then '3rd-4th'
        WHEN event_index <= 8   then '5th-8th'
        WHEN event_index <= 16  then '9th-16th'
        ELSE '17+'
    END AS event_index_grouping,
    total_matches,
    wins,
    submission_wins,
    losses,
    submission_losses
FROM
    event_indexed
ORDER BY
    athlete_id,
    event_index

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.events.name: events
model.smoothcomp_warehouse.events.description:
Event-level dataset built from scraped Smoothcomp event data.

This model standardizes event metadata and attaches the number of matches
associated with each event. It also derives several time attributes used
for time-series analysis and monthly aggregation.

**Grain:** One row per event.

**Key transformations:**
- Removes duplicate events from the raw scrape.
- Aggregates match data to compute total matches per event.
- Adds derived calendar fields for reporting and visualization.

**Upstream sources:**
- stg_events_raw
- stg_matches_raw

id.name: id
id.description: Unique identifier for the event scraped from Smoothcomp.

title.name: title
title.description: Title of the event as displayed on the event card in Smoothcomp search results.

href.name: href
href.description: URL linking to the event page on Smoothcomp.

location.name: location
location.description: Location of the event as listed in the event search results.

federation.name: federation
federation.description: Federation associated with the event, parsed from the event URL. Returns 'NA' when the event is not associated with a federation.

event_month.name: event_month
event_month.description: First day of the month corresponding to the event date, used for monthly aggregation.

month_num.name: month_num
month_num.description: Numeric month extracted from the event date (1–12).

month_name.name: month_name
month_name.description: Abbreviated month name derived from the event date (e.g., Jan, Feb, Mar). Implementation varies slightly by database engine.

match_count.name: match_count
match_count.description: Total number of matches recorded for the event based on aggregated match results from the raw matches dataset.

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.events.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized = 'table',
        format = 'parquet'
    )
}}

WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        events.id,
        events.title,
        events.href,
        events.location,
        events.federation,
        events.date,
        date_trunc('month', events.date) AS event_month,
        month(events.date) AS month_num,
        {% if target.type == 'duckdb' %}
            strftime('%b', events.date) AS month_name,
        {% else %}
            date_format(events.date, '%b') AS month_name,
        {% endif %}
        matches.match_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY events.id
            ORDER BY events.date DESC
        ) AS rn
    FROM {{ ref('stg_events_raw') }} events
    INNER JOIN (
        SELECT 
            event_id,
            SUM(win) AS match_count
        FROM {{ ref('stg_matches_raw') }}
        GROUP BY event_id
    ) matches
        ON matches.event_id = events.id
)

SELECT *
FROM base
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY date

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.matches_adult.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized='table',
        format='parquet'
    )
}}
SELECT
    event_id,
    match_id,
    athlete_id,
    name,
    club,
    win,
    submission_win,
    loss,
    submission_loss,
    style,
    age,
    gender,
    CASE
        WHEN m.skill = 'Absolute'
            THEN 'absolute'
        WHEN m.skill IN ('Advanced', 'Black')
            THEN 'black'
        WHEN m.skill = 'Brown'
            THEN 'brown'
        WHEN m.skill = 'Purple'
            THEN 'purple'
        WHEN m.skill IN ('Intermediate', 'Blue')
            THEN 'blue'
        WHEN m.skill IN ('White', 'Beginner')
            THEN 'white'
    END AS skill
FROM {{ ref('matches') }} m
WHERE
    m.style = 'Gi'
    AND m.age <> 'Youth'
    AND m.skill <> 'NA'

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.matches_by_cl.description:
Aggregated statistics of match performance by club within each event.

This model supports analysis of team participation and performance
across competitions.

Metrics include match counts and win/loss outcomes.

**Grain:** One row per club per event.

**Upstream model:**
- matches

match_count.description: Total matches participated in by club athletes.

wins.name: wins
wins.description: Total matches won by club athletes.

model.smoothcomp_warehouse.matches_by_cl.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized='table',
        format='parquet'
    )
}}
SELECT
    club,
    event_id,
    count(distinct athlete_id) as athlete_count,
    sum(win)+sum(loss) as match_count,
    sum(win) as wins,
    sum(submission_win) as submission_wins,
    sum(loss) as loss,
    sum(submission_loss) as submission_loss
FROM
    {{ ref('matches') }}
GROUP BY
    club,
    event_id

{{
    config(
        materialized='table',
        format='parquet'
    )
}}
SELECT
    event_month,
    style,
    count(distinct match_id) as match_count
FROM {{ ref('matches') }} m
LEFT JOIN

This dataset contains match records exactly as collected by the scraper

in Athena.

**Notes:**
- Categorical attributes such as age, gender, skill level, and style

- Match outcome indicators are captured per athlete perspective.

club.name: club

age.name: age

gender.name: gender

macro.dbt_duckdb.duckdb__list_relations_.name: duckdb__list_relations_without_caching
macro.dbt_duckdb.duckdb__list_relations_.macro_sql:
{% macro duckdb__list_relations_without_caching(schema_relation) %}
  {% call statement('list_relations_without_caching', fetch_result=True) -%}
    select
      '{{ schema_relation.database }}' as database,
      table_name as name,
      table_schema as schema,
      CASE table_type
        WHEN 'BASE TABLE' THEN 'table'
        WHEN 'VIEW' THEN 'view'
        WHEN 'LOCAL TEMPORARY' THEN 'table'
        END as type
    from system.information_schema.tables
    where lower(table_schema) = '{{ schema_relation.schema | lower }}'
    and lower(table_catalog) = '{{ schema_relation.database | lower }}'
  {% endcall %}
  {{ return(load_result('list_relations_without_caching').table) }}
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_duckdb.duckdb__get_catalog.name: duckdb__get_catalog
macro.dbt_duckdb.duckdb__get_catalog.macro_sql:
{% macro duckdb__get_catalog(information_schema, schemas) -%}
  {%- call statement('catalog', fetch_result=True) -%}
    with relations AS (
      select
        t.table_name
        , t.database_name
        , t.schema_name
        , 'BASE TABLE' as table_type
        , t.comment as table_comment
      from duckdb_tables() t
      WHERE t.database_name = '{{ database }}'
      UNION ALL
      SELECT v.view_name as table_name
      , v.database_name
      , v.schema_name
      , 'VIEW' as table_type
      , v.comment as table_comment
      from duckdb_views() v
      WHERE v.database_name = '{{ database }}'
    )
    select
        '{{ database }}' as table_database,
        r.schema_name as table_schema,
        r.table_name,
        r.table_type,
        r.table_comment,
        c.column_name,
        c.column_index as column_index,
        c.data_type as column_type,
        c.comment as column_comment,
        NULL as table_owner
    FROM relations r JOIN duckdb_columns() c ON r.schema_name = c.schema_name AND r.table_name = c.table_name
    WHERE (
        {%- for schema in schemas -%}
          upper(r.schema_name) = upper('{{ schema }}'){%- if not loop.last %} or {% endif -%}
        {%- endfor -%}
    )
    ORDER BY
        r.schema_name,
        r.table_name,
        c.column_index
  {%- endcall -%}
  {{ return(load_result('catalog').table) }}
{%- endmacro %}

doc.dbt.__overview__.block_contents:
### Welcome!

Welcome to the auto-generated documentation for your dbt project!

### Navigation

You can use the `Project` and `Database` navigation tabs on the left side of the window to explore the models
in your project.

#### Project Tab
The `Project` tab mirrors the directory structure of your dbt project. In this tab, you can see all of the
models defined in your dbt project, as well as models imported from dbt packages.

#### Database Tab
The `Database` tab also exposes your models, but in a format that looks more like a database explorer. This view
shows relations (tables and views) grouped into database schemas. Note that ephemeral models are _not_ shown
in this interface, as they do not exist in the database.

### Graph Exploration
You can click the blue icon on the bottom-right corner of the page to view the lineage graph of your models.

On model pages, you'll see the immediate parents and children of the model you're exploring. By clicking the `Expand`
button at the top-right of this lineage pane, you'll be able to see all of the models that are used to build,
or are built from, the model you're exploring.

Once expanded, you'll be able to use the `--select` and `--exclude` model selection syntax to filter the
models in the graph. For more information on model selection, check out the [dbt docs](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/model-selection-syntax).

Note that you can also right-click on models to interactively filter and explore the graph.

---

### More information

- Read the [dbt viewpoint](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/viewpoint)
- [Installation](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/installation)
- Join the [dbt Community](https://www.getdbt.com/community/) for questions and discussion
